page.title: Pershing 46 Charter Review: What It's Like Onboard in Marbella

data.meta_description: Read our honest Pershing 46 Marbella review: speed, comfort, and onboard experience from Puerto Banús. Specs, pricing, and what to expect.
data.summary: An honest review of the Pershing 46 charter experience in Marbella, covering speed, comfort, onboard amenities, and local cruising grounds from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Top speed approx. 38 knots – one of our fastest flybridge models.
Onboard: sunbathing area, saloon with galley, two cabins.
Charter includes skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and 21% VAT.
Pricing on request via WhatsApp – no set hourly rates.
Ideal for day trips to Cabopino, Estepona, or Cala Cortés.
Built by Pershing – Italian design and performance pedigree.

faq.a: The Pershing 46 cruises at around 30–35 knots and can reach a top speed of approximately 38 knots, depending on sea conditions and load. It’s one of the faster boats in our fleet, ideal for covering ground quickly or just enjoying the thrill of speed along the Marbella coast.

faq.q: How many people can the Pershing 46 hold?
faq.a: The Pershing 46 is licensed for up to 10 passengers. This includes the skipper, so you can bring up to 9 guests. It’s a good fit for small groups of friends or family who want a sporty yet comfortable day out.

faq.q: What is included in the Pershing 46 charter?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no hidden extras.

faq.q: Does the Pershing 46 have a cabin?
faq.a: Yes, the Pershing 46 has two cabins below deck, plus a saloon with a small galley. It’s not a liveaboard for extended trips, but it provides comfortable shelter and a place to change or rest during a day charter.

faq.q: Where does the Pershing 46 depart from?
faq.a: The Pershing 46 departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s iconic marina. From there, you can head east toward Cabopino or west to Estepona, Sotogrande, or the quieter coves like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés.

faq.q: How much does it cost to charter the Pershing 46?
faq.a: Pricing for the Pershing 46 is on request via WhatsApp. Unlike some of our fixed-price boats (e.g., Astondoa 40 at €749/2h), the Pershing is custom-quoted based on duration, season, and itinerary. Contact us for a specific quote.

faq.q: Is the Pershing 46 suitable for a wedding or special event?
faq.a: Yes, it works well for small celebrations like a wedding proposal, birthday, or anniversary. The sporty profile and stylish interior impress guests, and the flybridge offers a great vantage point for photos. Just note the 10-pax limit.

faq.q: What is the best itinerary for a Pershing 46 day charter?
faq.a: A popular route is heading west from Puerto Banús to Estepona (about 20 minutes at cruising speed), with a swim stop at Cala Cortés. Alternatively, go east to Cabopino and anchor for lunch. Your skipper will tailor the route to your preferences.

data.body_html:
If you’re looking for a sporty, stylish, and fast boat to explore the Marbella coast, the Pershing 46 is a standout choice. Built by the Italian yard known for combining performance with luxury, this 14-metre flybridge cruiser offers a genuine thrill behind the wheel without sacrificing comfort. In this Pershing 46 Marbella review, we’ll walk you through the onboard experience, the practical details, and what makes this boat a favourite among guests departing from Puerto Banús.

First Impressions: Design and Build

The Pershing 46 cuts a sharp profile. With its low-slung hull, aggressive bow, and sleek flybridge, it looks fast even at the dock. The build quality is unmistakably Italian—every surface, from the teak decking to the stainless steel fittings, feels premium. The cockpit is spacious for a boat of this size, with a large sunbed aft and a dinette area that converts into an additional lounging space. The flybridge is compact but well laid out, with a helm station that puts you in control and a small seating area for guests who want to ride up top.

Onboard Comfort: Cabins, Saloon, and Galley

Below deck, the Pershing 46 sleeps up to four guests in two cabins. The master cabin is forward with a double berth, while the VIP cabin has twin single beds. Both are finished in light wood and cream upholstery, creating a bright, airy feel. The saloon is compact but functional, with a small galley equipped with a sink, fridge, and hob—enough for preparing snacks and drinks during the day. The head is clean and well-sized, with a separate shower. While you wouldn’t live aboard for a week, it’s perfectly comfortable for day charters, offering a cool retreat from the Mediterranean sun.

Performance and Handling

The Pershing 46 is powered by twin diesel engines that push it to a top speed of around 38 knots. Cruising at 30–35 knots is smooth and stable, with minimal vibration even in moderate chop. The hull is deep-V, which cuts through the sea cleanly, and the steering is responsive—exactly what you’d expect from a brand that builds performance boats. For a day charter, this means you can cover more ground: from Puerto Banús to Estepona in under 20 minutes, or zip out to Cabopino for a swim stop and still have time for a leisurely lunch. The skipper will handle all the navigation, so you can just enjoy the ride.

Charter Inclusions and Pricing

Chartering the Pershing 46 includes everything you need for a day on the water: a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and a full drinks package (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava) plus light snacks. Spanish IVA at 21% is included in the price. Unlike some of our fixed-price boats—such as the Astondoa 40 at €749 for 2 hours—the Pershing 46 is priced on request. This is because itineraries, duration, and season all affect the rate. To get a specific quote, just contact us via WhatsApp and we’ll tailor a package to your group. There are no hidden costs: what we quote is what you pay.

Best Itineraries from Puerto Banús

With its speed, the Pershing 46 opens up the entire western Costa del Sol. A classic day trip heads west to Estepona, stopping at Cala Cortés or Cala del Faro for a swim in crystal-clear water. From there, you can continue to Sotogrande for lunch at a beach club, or anchor in the bay. Alternatively, go east to Cabopino, a quieter spot with a sandy beach and a handful of chiringuitos. The skipper will adapt the route to your preferences, whether you want a fast sightseeing tour or a relaxed day of swimming and sunbathing. For a shorter trip, staying close to the Golden Mile and Marbella Marina is also popular.

Who Is the Pershing 46 Best For?

The Pershing 46 suits groups of up to 10 guests who want a sporty, modern boat with a bit of edge. It’s ideal for couples, small families, or a group of friends looking for a memorable day out. The speed appeals to those who enjoy the thrill of planing across the water, while the comfortable interior and sunbathing areas keep everyone happy. It’s also a strong choice for special occasions—think a birthday, anniversary, or even a small wedding celebration. Just keep the 10-pax limit in mind. If you need more space, consider one of our larger models like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or the Mangusta 80.

What Guests Frequently Ask

How fast is the Pershing 46?

The Pershing 46 cruises at around 30–35 knots and can reach a top speed of approximately 38 knots, depending on sea conditions and load. It’s one of the faster boats in our fleet, ideal for covering ground quickly or just enjoying the thrill of speed along the Marbella coast.

How many people can the Pershing 46 hold?

The Pershing 46 is licensed for up to 10 passengers. This includes the skipper, so you can bring up to 9 guests. It’s a good fit for small groups of friends or family who want a sporty yet comfortable day out.

What is included in the Pershing 46 charter?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no hidden extras.

Does the Pershing 46 have a cabin?

Yes, the Pershing 46 has two cabins below deck, plus a saloon with a small galley. It’s not a liveaboard for extended trips, but it provides comfortable shelter and a place to change or rest during a day charter.

Where does the Pershing 46 depart from?

The Pershing 46 departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s iconic marina. From there, you can head east toward Cabopino or west to Estepona, Sotogrande, or the quieter coves like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés.

How much does it cost to charter the Pershing 46?

Pricing for the Pershing 46 is on request via WhatsApp. Unlike some of our fixed-price boats (e.g., Astondoa 40 at €749/2h), the Pershing is custom-quoted based on duration, season, and itinerary. Contact us for a specific quote.

Is the Pershing 46 suitable for a wedding or special event?

Yes, it works well for small celebrations like a wedding proposal, birthday, or anniversary. The sporty profile and stylish interior impress guests, and the flybridge offers a great vantage point for photos. Just note the 10-pax limit.

What is the best itinerary for a Pershing 46 day charter?

A popular route is heading west from Puerto Banús to Estepona (about 20 minutes at cruising speed), with a swim stop at Cala Cortés. Alternatively, go east to Cabopino and anchor for lunch. Your skipper will tailor the route to your preferences.

Ready to experience the Pershing 46 for yourself? Get in touch via WhatsApp for a personalised quote, and we’ll help you plan an unforgettable day on the water. For more inspiration, browse our charter guides or check out the full Boat Rental Marbella fleet.
